sources.notes: Operations replace a neighboring unequal pair with an equal pair; the goal is to transform a parenthesis string into a regular bracket sequence.
Official reference code computes an alternating signed balance invariant and directly constructs a target regular bracket sequence when possible.

annotation.summary: Decide whether a bracket string can be transformed into a regular bracket sequence by repeatedly changing adjacent different brackets into equal brackets, and construct one if possible.
annotation.constraints: n is large, so the answer must be based on a linear invariant and direct construction.
annotation.core_idea: The operation preserves an alternating signed balance of the string. Any reachable regular bracket sequence must have the same invariant, which gives a necessary condition. That condition is also sufficient: construct a regular bracket sequence with the required alternating balance by placing one outer pair, a controlled number of alternating pairs inside it, and the remaining pairs afterward; handle the all-closing extreme as a special construction.

solution_variants.name: invariant-construction
solution_variants.summary: Check the preserved alternating balance and build a regular bracket sequence with that balance.

tags.evidence: After deriving the invariant, the solution explicitly constructs a valid target bracket sequence.
The reachability condition is the alternating signed balance preserved by every allowed operation.
The object being transformed is a bracket string, and the construction is a linear string construction.
The construction needs a separate treatment for the extreme case where the invariant forces all closing positions in the template.
